bloatware.name: Cortana
bloatware.description: Voice assistant - consumes significant resources
bloatware.notes: Preinstalled in all Windows 10/11 versions

bloatware.name: OneDrive
bloatware.description: Cloud storage - can be disabled without risk
bloatware.notes: Preinstalled, runs at startup automatically

bloatware.name: Microsoft Edge
bloatware.description: Browser - Chromium-based, resource-heavy
bloatware.notes: System app, difficult to remove on Windows 11. WebView2 runtime used by many apps.

bloatware.name: Microsoft Copilot
bloatware.description: AI chatbot sidebar - background telemetry, opens by default
bloatware.notes: Pushed via Windows Update. No critical system dependencies.

bloatware.name: Candy Crush Saga
bloatware.description: Pre-installed advertising-supported game - pure bloatware
bloatware.notes: Pre-installed depending on Windows edition. Part of Microsoft/King partnership.

bloatware.name: Candy Crush Soda Saga
bloatware.description: Pre-installed advertising-supported game - pure bloatware
bloatware.notes: Pre-installed depending on Windows edition.

bloatware.name: Microsoft Teams (Consumer)
bloatware.description: Consumer Teams pre-installed, runs at startup via taskbar Chat icon
bloatware.notes: Confirm before removing in corporate environments. Work/school accounts may require it.

bloatware.name: Skype
bloatware.description: Communication app - pre-installed, registers itself at startup
bloatware.notes: No system dependencies. Safe to remove.

bloatware.name: Spotify
bloatware.description: Music app - bundled by some OEMs, runs background update processes
bloatware.notes: No system dependencies. Check %AppData%\Spotify for leftover folders.

bloatware.name: Windows Mail
bloatware.description: Built-in mail client - replaced by most users with alternatives
bloatware.notes: May be pinned in Start. Replace with preferred client before removing.

bloatware.name: Windows Photos
bloatware.description: Built-in photo viewer - replaced by most users with alternatives
bloatware.notes: Default image viewer. Replace with IrfanView or similar before removing.

bloatware.name: Movies & TV
bloatware.description: Built-in media player - replaced by VLC or similar
bloatware.notes: No critical OS dependencies.

bloatware.name: Xbox App
bloatware.description: Xbox platform app - background identity services even when unused
bloatware.notes: Some UWP games depend on Xbox identity services. May break Xbox Live sign-in.

bloatware.name: Xbox Game Bar
bloatware.description: Win+G overlay - background processes even if unused
bloatware.notes: Can be disabled only via Settings > Gaming > Xbox Game Bar instead of full removal.

bloatware.name: McAfee Security
bloatware.description: OEM-bundled antivirus trial - resource-heavy, hard to remove via normal means
bloatware.notes: Pre-installed on many OEM machines (HP, Dell, Lenovo). Use MCPR.exe tool, not standard uninstaller. Windows Defender re-enables automatically after removal.

bloatware.name: Norton Security
bloatware.description: OEM-bundled antivirus trial - intrusive, difficult to uninstall cleanly
bloatware.notes: Pre-installed on many OEM machines. Use Norton Remove and Reinstall Tool (NRnR), not standard uninstaller. Verify Windows Defender re-enables after removal.

bloatware.name: HP Support Assistant
bloatware.description: HP OEM utility - runs background agents, auto-launches, phones home
bloatware.notes: Pre-installed on HP machines only. HP Wolf Security is separate - consult IT before removing on managed devices.

bloatware.name: Dell SupportAssist
bloatware.description: Dell OEM utility - background telemetry and update agent
bloatware.notes: Pre-installed on Dell machines only. Dell warranty diagnostics unavailable after removal. Drivers must be managed manually via Dell support site.

bloatware.name: Lenovo Vantage
bloatware.description: Lenovo OEM hardware management suite - background agents, telemetry, past security vulnerabilities
bloatware.notes: Pre-installed on Lenovo machines only. Removing means manual driver updates via Lenovo support site. Fn hotkeys may still work via firmware.

bloatware.name: ASUS Armory Crate
bloatware.description: ASUS gaming software suite - multiple background processes, lighting control, high memory footprint
bloatware.notes: Pre-installed on ASUS machines. Use ASUS Armory Crate Uninstall Tool from ASUS support site. RGB lighting defaults to static/off after removal. History of supply-chain vulnerability (ShadowHammer 2019).

bloatware.name: Acer Care Center
bloatware.description: Acer OEM utility suite - background diagnostics, product upsell
bloatware.notes: Pre-installed on Acer machines only. Brightness/volume/airplane mode hotkeys may stop working on some Acer models without Quick Access component.

bloatware.name: WildTangent Games
bloatware.description: OEM game launcher - pre-installed on HP/Dell/Acer, pure advertising bloatware
bloatware.notes: Bundled by various OEMs. No services, just files and registry entries. Fully safe to remove.

bloatware.name: Riot Vanguard
bloatware.description: Anti-cheat kernel driver - installs as a service, runs at boot even when not gaming
bloatware.notes: Installed alongside Riot Games titles (Valorant, League of Legends). vgk.sys is a kernel-level driver. Removing disables ability to play Riot titles until reinstalled.

bloatware.name: Adware Toolbars (Ask, Babylon, Conduit)
bloatware.description: Browser hijackers - hijack search defaults, homepage, and new-tab page
bloatware.notes: Often bundled with freeware installers. Reset browser after removal. Run Malwarebytes AdwCleaner for thorough cleanup.
